term_inventory_greek.meaning: In fear, terrified, affrighted; thrown into fear; used of persons seized with terror at a supernatural appearance.
To revere, worship, honour religiously; in NT used especially of Gentile God-fearers attached to the Jewish synagogue; to worship God, feel religious awe.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: sebomai: to feel awe or shame before; to revere, worship; used of Gentile adherents to the Jewish synagogue (God-fearers).

term_inventory_greek.meaning: To be timid, cowardly, fearful; used only once in NT as a prohibition against cowardly fear in contrast to the courage that comes from the indwelling Spirit.

Devout, cautious in religious matters, circumspect; in NT always refers to devout persons characterised by reverential fear of God: Simeon (Lk 2:25), the Jews of the diaspora (Act 2:5), those who buried Stephen (Act 8:2). Meaning block lists 4 occurrences including Act 22:12 but that verse is absent from the verse section.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: eulabes: cautious, wary, discreet; in religious sense, devout, God-fearing.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Fear, reverential awe; in NT hapax at Heb 12:28 in the phrase 'with reverence and awe' (meta eulabeias kai deous). Manuscript variants exist.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: deos: fear, awe, reverence; in Classical Greek especially reverential awe before the gods or the sacred.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Fear in its full range: (1) alarm, terror, the emotion produced by perceived threat or danger; (2) reverential awe, deep respect, the proper attitude toward God and those in authority; (3) the object that causes fear. Covers both the psychological state and its outward expression.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: fobos: fear, terror, alarm; also reverential fear, awe, respect. Active sense: that which causes fear, a terror. In NT both fear of threat and reverential fear of God.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Consternation, dismay, a frightening thing; something alarming; used in 1 Pet 3:6 in the negative - women who do not fear anything that is frightening.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: ptoesis: vehement emotion or excitement; consternation, dismay, a frightening thing.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Fearful, terrible, dreadful; describes God or divine judgment as inspiring awe and terror. All three NT occurrences are in Hebrews and refer to the fearsome character of God's judgment.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: foberos: fearful, terrible, formidable; inspiring fear or awe.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: A fearful thing, a terror, something that produces fear; in NT used of signs or portents that cause dread (Lk 21:11) - earthquakes, famines, and fearful sights preceding the end.

Fearlessly, without fear; in NT used to describe conduct free from fear - serving God without fear (Lk 1:74), speaking boldly (Phil 1:14; 1 Cor 16:10), and in a negative sense, false teachers who feast without restraint (Jude 12).
God-fearing, devout, worshipping God; in NT used once of the man born blind's defense of Jesus: 'If anyone is a worshiper of God and does his will, God listens to him' (Jn 9:31).
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: theosebes: God-fearing, devout, pious; worshipping God.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Reverence, godly fear, pious awe; cautious, devout disposition toward God; in NT used of Christ's prayers offered with reverence and godly fear (Heb 5:7) and of acceptable worship offered with eulabeia and deos (Heb 12:28).
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: eulabeia: caution, circumspection; in religious sense, reverence, godly fear, piety.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Utterly terrified, terrified out of one's wits; an intensified compound form expressing complete terror - used of Moses at the theophany on Sinai (Heb 12:21) and of the disciples at the Transfiguration (Mk 9:6).
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: ekfobos: frightened out of one's wits, utterly terrified.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Cowardly, timid, fearful; characterised by lack of courage; in NT used of the disciples in the storm (Mt 8:26; Mk 4:40) rebuked by Jesus, and in Rev 21:8 the cowardly are listed among those excluded from the new Jerusalem.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: deilos: cowardly, timid, faint-hearted; in moral sense, lacking courage.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Cowardice, timidity; in NT used only in 2 Tim 1:7 in the direct contrast with the Spirit's gift: 'God gave us a spirit not of fear (deilia) but of power and love and self-control.' Carries the specific sense of craven fear, the opposite of Spirit-given courage.

To have holy fear, to reverence; to be moved by reverential awe; in NT used of Noah who, being warned by God, acted in reverent fear (Heb 11:7); and of the tribune who feared Paul would be torn apart (Act 23:10 - secular sense of anxiety).
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: eulabeomai: to be discreet, cautious, beware; to take care; to reverence, pay honour to God; to fear, be apprehensive.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: To fear, be afraid, alarmed; to reverence, respect, worship; active form means to terrify but occurs in NT only as passive/deponent. Wide semantic range: alarm at natural danger, fear of persons, reverential fear of God, reluctance to act, anxious apprehension.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: fobeo, active: to put to flight, terrify, alarm. Passive/middle (predominant in NT): to be afraid, to be seized with fear; to fear reverentially, to reverence, worship; to be reluctant, scruple; to be fearfully anxious.

term_inventory_greek.meaning: Trembling, terrified; physically shaking with fear; in NT used of Moses trembling at the theophany (Act 7:32; Heb 12:21) and the Philippian jailer who rushed in trembling with fear before Paul and Silas (Act 16:29). Somatic component explicit.

Trembling, quaking from fear; the physical manifestation of fear or solemn awe; in NT predominantly in the phrase 'fear and trembling' (fobos kai tromos) marking reverential seriousness. Mk 16:8 uses it of the women at the empty tomb.
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: tromos: trembling, quaking; from fear; from cold; of earthquakes.

cross_registry_links.note: de.a.gah (anxiety H1674) appears in both the fear and sorrow word sets as a cross-reference term.
quts (to loathe H6973) appears in both the fear and distress word sets as a cross-reference term.
chul (to twist: tremble H2342A) appears in both the fear and anguish word sets as a cross-reference term.
ro.gez (turmoil H7267) appears in both the fear and anger word sets as a cross-reference term.

data_quality_flags.description: katafobos (G7949): heading present in source file but word analysis block contained duplicate ptoesis (G4423) content. STEP confirmed 0 ESV occurrences. Term excluded per researcher decision (Q1 Option A, 2026-03-11).
foberizō (G5420): heading present in source file but no word analysis block or verses. Term excluded per researcher decision (Q2, 2026-03-11).
devout (eulabes G2126): meaning block lists 4 occurrences including Act 22:12, but Act 22:12 is absent from the verse section. 3 verses extracted. Occurrence count recorded as 3 from verses present.
to fear (fobeo G5399): ~94 occurrences stated; 78 verses extracted. Verses are less than 20% threshold is not met. All verses in source file extracted.

fear (deos): source file lists G6015 which is non-standard. Researcher screenshot confirmed G7949 = katafobos (excluded). Correct Strong's for deos hapax (Heb 12:28) unconfirmed. Term recorded under G1169A (MTI provisional). Researcher to verify via STEP before Session B.
